sections.heading: Real Numbers & Progressions
sections.body: Natural Numbers (1, 2, 3...), Whole Numbers (0, 1, 2...), Integers (...-2, -1, 0, 1, 2...), Rational Numbers (expressible as p/q), and Irrational Numbers (non-terminating, non-repeating decimals like √2 and π). HCF is the largest factor common to two or more numbers. LCM is the smallest multiple common to two or more numbers. HCF × LCM = Product of the two numbers. Arithmetic Progression (AP) is a sequence where the difference between consecutive terms is constant. The nth term a_n = a + (n-1)d.

sections.heading: Algebra and Quadratic Equations
sections.body: A linear equation in two variables (ax + by + c = 0) represents a straight line with infinitely many solutions. A system of two linear equations can have one unique solution (intersecting lines), no solution (parallel lines), or infinite solutions (coincident lines). A quadratic equation (ax² + bx + c = 0) has at most two roots, found by factoring or the quadratic formula: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a. The discriminant (b²-4ac) determines the nature of roots: positive = two real roots, zero = one repeated root, negative = no real roots.

sections.heading: Trigonometry & Mensuration
sections.body: Trigonometric ratios for a right-angled triangle: sin θ = Opposite/Hypotenuse, cos θ = Adjacent/Hypotenuse, tan θ = Opposite/Adjacent. Standard values: sin 0° = 0, sin 30° = 1/2, sin 45° = 1/v2, sin 60° = v3/2, sin 90° = 1. Pythagoras theorem states that in a right-angled triangle, a² + b² = c² (where c is the hypotenuse). Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. Volume of a cone = (1/3)πr²h. Volume of a sphere = (4/3)πr³.

sections.heading: Physics: Light and Electricity
sections.body: The laws of reflection are valid for all reflecting surfaces. Concave mirrors form real/virtual and magnified/diminished images depending on object position. Convex mirrors always form virtual, erect, and diminished images. Refractive index is the ratio of the speed of light in vacuum to that in a medium. Ohm's Law: V = IR (Voltage = Current × Resistance). Ammeters measure current and are connected in series. Voltmeters measure potential difference and are connected in parallel.

sections.heading: Chemistry: Acids, Bases and Carbon Compounds
sections.body: Acids have pH < 7 and turn blue litmus paper red. Bases have pH > 7 and turn red litmus blue. Neutral solutions have pH 7. Quicklime is Calcium Oxide (CaO), Slaked lime is Calcium Hydroxide (Ca(OH)₂). Carbon has atomic number 6 and forms covalent bonds through tetravalency. Saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) contain single C-C bonds, while unsaturated ones (alkenes, alkynes) contain double or triple bonds.

sections.heading: Biology: Nutrition, Respiration & Coordination
sections.body: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light -> C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Occurs in chloroplasts containing chlorophyll. Respiration is aerobic (with oxygen, releasing CO₂ and ATP) or anaerobic (without oxygen). The human heart has 4 chambers. Kidney filtration units are nephrons. Brain and spinal cord make up the Central Nervous System. Genetics principles were founded by Gregor Mendel using pea plants.

sections.heading: Geography: India's Relief & Rivers
sections.body: Kanchenjunga is the highest peak in India. Godavari is called Dakshin Ganga. India has a Tropical Monsoon climate. The Standard Meridian of India is 82.5° E, passing through Mirzapur in UP. Black soil (Regur) is excellent for cotton. The Ganga basin is the largest river basin in India. Western coastal plains have Malabar Coast in the south and Konkan Coast in the north.

sections.heading: History & Civics
sections.body: First PM was Jawaharlal Nehru. Constitution was adopted on 26 Nov 1949 and came into effect on 26 Jan 1950. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Drafting Committee chairman. Quit India Movement was launched in 1942. World War I ended in 1919 with the Treaty of Versailles. United Nations was created in 1945. Supreme Court is the final interpreter of the Indian Constitution.

sections.heading: Economics & Development
sections.body: Primary sector includes agriculture and mining. Secondary sector covers manufacturing. Tertiary sector represents services like banking. GDP is the total monetary value of goods/services produced. Sustainable development balances economic growth and environmental preservation. WTO regulates international trade rules. Right to Information (RTI) was enacted in 2005.

sections.heading: English Grammar
sections.body: Nouns name entities, Adverbs modify verbs/adjectives (e.g. beautifully). Active voice shows subject performing action; passive voice shows subject receiving it (e.g. A letter was written by him). 'Since' denotes a point in time, 'for' denotes duration. Either/or verb agreement goes with the closer subject. Coordinating conjunctions include but, and, or. Similes compare using like/as; metaphors compare directly.

sections.heading: Telugu and Hindi Fundamentals
sections.body: Telugu Adi Kavi Nannayya wrote Mahabharatam. In Telugu, vowels are Achulu (అచ్చులు) and consonants are Hallulu (హల్లులు). Ubhayaksharalu are Sunna, Ara-sunna, and Visarga. Hindi is written in Devanagari script. Vowels in Hindi are Swar (स्वर). Ugadi is the lunisolar New Year of Andhra/Telangana/Karnataka. National Song 'Vande Mataram' was composed by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay. National Anthem 'Jana Gana Mana' was composed in Bengali by Rabindranath Tagore.
